questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --enablerepo= option temporarily enable additional repositories for the purpose of the current dnf command.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --enablerepo= option temporarily enable additional repositories for the purpose of the current dnf command.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --enablerepo= option include enhancement relevant packages.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --enablerepo= option temporarily enable additional repositories for the purpose of the current dnf command.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --enhancement option include enhancement relevant packages.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --enhancement option include enhancement relevant packages.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --enhancement option exclude packages specified by <package-file-spec> from the operation.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --enhancement option include enhancement relevant packages.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` -x option exclude packages specified by <package-file-spec> from the operation.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` -x option exclude packages specified by <package-file-spec> from the operation.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` -x option it was replaced by the - -exclude option.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` -x option exclude packages specified by <package-file-spec> from the operation.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --excludepkgs= option it was replaced by the - -exclude option.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --excludepkgs= option it was replaced by the - -exclude option.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --excludepkgs= option force the use of an architecture.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --excludepkgs= option it was replaced by the - -exclude option.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --forcearch= option force the use of an architecture.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --forcearch= option force the use of an architecture.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --forcearch= option specifies an alternative installroot, relative to where all packages will be installed.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --forcearch= option force the use of an architecture.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option specifies an alternative installroot, relative to where all packages will be installed.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option specifies an alternative installroot, relative to where all packages will be installed.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option permanently sets the releasever of the system in the <installroot> directory to <release>.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option specifies an alternative installroot, relative to where all packages will be installed.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option permanently sets the releasever of the system in the <installroot> directory to <release> .
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option permanently sets the releasever of the system in the <installroot> directory to <release>.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option upgrades packages inside the installroot from a repository described by --setopt using configuration from /path/dnf.conf.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option permanently sets the releasever of the system in the <installroot> directory to <release>.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option upgrades packages inside the installroot from a repository described by --setopt using configuration from /path/dnf.conf .
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option upgrades packages inside the installroot from a repository described by --setopt using configuration from /path/dnf.conf.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --installroot= option include newpackage relevant packages.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --installroot= option upgrades packages inside the installroot from a repository described by --setopt using configuration from /path/dnf.conf.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --newpackage option include newpackage relevant packages.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --newpackage option include newpackage relevant packages.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --newpackage option disables removal of dependencies that are no longer used.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --newpackage option include newpackage relevant packages.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --noautoremove option disables removal of dependencies that are no longer used.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --noautoremove option disables removal of dependencies that are no longer used.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --noautoremove option sets best option to False , so that transactions are not limited to best candidates only.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --noautoremove option disables removal of dependencies that are no longer used.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nobest option sets best option to False , so that transactions are not limited to best candidates only.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --nobest option sets best option to False , so that transactions are not limited to best candidates only.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nobest option does not install documentation.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --nobest option sets best option to False , so that transactions are not limited to best candidates only.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nodocs option does not install documentation.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --nodocs option does not install documentation.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nodocs option skip checking GPG signatures on packages (if RPM policy allows).
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --nodocs option does not install documentation.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nogpgcheck option skip checking GPG signatures on packages (if RPM policy allows).
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --nogpgcheck option skip checking GPG signatures on packages (if RPM policy allows).

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --nogpgcheck option this option has an effect on an install/update, it enables dnf’s obsoletes processing logic.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --nogpgcheck option skip checking GPG signatures on packages (if RPM policy allows).

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --obsoletes option this option has an effect on an install/update, it enables dnf’s obsoletes processing logic.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` --obsoletes option this option has an effect on an install/update, it enables dnf’s obsoletes processing logic.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` --obsoletes option in combination with a non-interactive command, shows just the relevant content.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` --obsoletes option this option has an effect on an install/update, it enables dnf’s obsoletes processing logic.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` -q option in combination with a non-interactive command, shows just the relevant content.
questions.explanation: Correct. `dnf command ref` -q option in combination with a non-interactive command, shows just the relevant content.

questions.question: The `dnf command ref` -q option maximum command wait time.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `dnf command ref` -q option in combination with a non-interactive command, shows just the relevant content.
